category_title: Students who prioritize learning and demonstrating ability through effective strategy use
scenario_title: Reading comprehension task with known disorganization
minimal_context: The student is working on a reading comprehension task about soccer.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Here is the reading comprehension task about soccer. Please read the text and answer the questions.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I thought that this was interesting…the task was fun. I'm going to turn this into a game to see how many answers I can find, because I want to get a high grade and show I can do it.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, go ahead and start working on it.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I know I have some difficulties sometimes, so I am using my strategies to plan and organize. I am marking the main ideas in the paragraphs as I read.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think you should do next?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I really want to master this task, but my organizing strategy isn't working. I marked way too many things and now my paper is completely disorganized, so I can't figure out how to actually answer the questions.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Here is the reading comprehension task about soccer. Please read the text and answer the questions.

STUDENT: I thought that this was interesting…the task was fun. I'm going to turn this into a game to see how many answers I can find, because I want to get a high grade and show I can do it.

TUTOR: Okay, go ahead and start working on it.

STUDENT: I know I have some difficulties sometimes, so I am using my strategies to plan and organize. I am marking the main ideas in the paragraphs as I read.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: I really want to master this task, but my organizing strategy isn't working. I marked way too many things and now my paper is completely disorganized, so I can't figure out how to actually answer the questions.
